compiled_state.title: As a developer, my commits are not blocked by resource exhaustion failures
compiled_state.author: Main
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Update pre-commit-test-gate.sh and all existing parsers of test-gate-status to accept the new resource_exhaustion status as non-blocking
**Why**: Without this change, the test gate blocks commits when resource exhaustion is recorded, even though the test logic itself passed or was not meaningfully tested
**Scope**:
- IN: Update fast-path check (~line 471) and full-path check (~line 594) in pre-commit-test-gate.sh, update validate.sh and record-test-status.sh to handle the new status value without error
- OUT: Suite-engine retry logic (separate story), record-test-status.sh EAGAIN detection (separate story)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, pre-commit-test-gate.sh treats resource_exhaustion status as non-blocking at both check points (fast-path and full-path)
  ← Satisfies: "The test gate treats resource_exhaustion status as non-blocking at both check points"
- When this story is complete, resource_exhaustion is visible as a distinct status value in test-gate-status output, and all existing parsers (validate.sh, pre-commit-test-gate.sh, record-test-status.sh) handle it without error
  ← Satisfies: "Resource exhaustion events are visible in test-gate-status output as a distinct resource_exhaustion status value"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Downstream parsers that use exact-match for passed/failed must be updated to handle the third status — grep for all consumers of test-gate-status before implementing
